reasoning: On March 20, the market probability for this question was 94%. At that time, Bayern Munich had just drawn against Union Berlin, reducing their lead over Bayer Leverkusen to 6 points with 8 matches remaining. As of March 29, 2025, Bayern defeated St. Pauli 3-2, while Leverkusen beat Bochum 3-1, meaning the 6-point gap remains, but now with only 7 matches left to play. Bayern Munich also possesses a vastly superior goal difference, which effectively serves as a 7-point gap. With only 21 points left on the board, Leverkusen would have to win nearly every remaining match while hoping Bayern drops points in at least three games. Given Bayern's historical dominance, their current form, and the shrinking number of remaining fixtures, their chances of holding on to win the title have improved since the March 20 market baseline. Therefore, the probability is estimated at 96%.
